title: Remove Redundant Boolean Literals
description: Redundant boolean literals can make the code harder to read and understand. This rule identifies instances where a boolean literal is compared to a variable or expression that evaluates to a boolean value, which is unnecessary. Simplifying these expressions can improve code readability and reduce the likelihood of bugs.
recommendation: Identify the boolean expressions where literals are compared with variables or other expressions. Simplify these expressions by removing the unnecessary comparisons with boolean literals.
impact: Improves code readability and understandability, reducing the likelihood of introducing new bugs.

checks.message: Identify the boolean expressions where literals are compared with variables or other expressions. Simplify these expressions by removing the unnecessary comparisons with boolean literals.
